title: Premna angustior
taxonomy: Premna angustior is classified as a species within the genus Premna, which belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The Lamiaceae family is commonly known as the mint family. This classification places Premna angustior in the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that share similar characteristics. The family Lamiaceae is part of the larger clade called asterids, which also includes other flowering plant families. The species is native to tropical regions, further specifying its classification within the plant kingdom.

intro: Premna angustior, a member of the Lamiaceae family, is a plant species commonly known as 'tongkat ali' or 'long jack', characterized by its woody stems and small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to enhance testosterone levels, improve bone density, and boost immune function. The bioactive constituents of Premna angustior include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Premna angustior is typically prepared as a capsule or supplement, often in combination with other herbal ingredients, to support men's health and well-being.
